hypothesis: If amorphous structures are generated using AMDEN—an EGNN-based diffusion model with VE/VP SDEs, energy-guided scoring, and HMC refinement—then their RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions will match those of MD-generated amorphous silicon across different thermal histories and, when conditioned on Young’s modulus, the model will produce multi-element glasses achieving the specified modulus because the equivariant score and energy guidance learn and sample from the joint distribution of atomic positions and compositions while favoring equilibrated configurations.
